# fluttersdk_dusk

> E2E driver for Flutter apps targeting LLM agents and CI. 34 CLI commands and 33 MCP tools drive a running Flutter app over 30 `ext.dusk.*` VM Service extensions plus 3 `artisan:dusk:*` substrate-routed tools. Every gesture passes a 6-step actionability gate (defunct, enabled, zero-rect, off-viewport, stable, receives-events) with substring-parseable failure reasons. Snapshots emit YAML trees with stable `[ref=eN]` tokens; `dusk:find` mints re-resolvable `q<N>` predicate handles (Playwright Locator pattern). Debug-only at the consumer call site; release builds tree-shake the entire subsystem.

**MCP tools (33)**, see `doc/mcp/tool-reference.md` for full input schemas. The 30 `ext.dusk.*` and 3 `artisan:dusk:*` (substrate-routed) tools are listed below.

*Snapshot / Screenshot*
- `dusk_snap` (`ext.dusk.snap`): Capture Semantics tree YAML with stable `[ref=eN]` tokens. Nodes inside a currently-overflowing ancestor carry an `overflow: true` sub-line. Nested `textbox` nodes collapse by render-object containment to a single ref tagged `typeable: true` (the node `dusk_type` resolves); sibling fields sharing a label stay distinct.
- `dusk_screenshot` (`ext.dusk.screenshot`, in-isolate): Capture base64-encoded JPEG/PNG frame. Can hang on web under CanvasKit+DWDS; for reliable web screenshots use the CLI `dusk:screenshot --output=<path>`, which falls back to CDP `Page.captureScreenshot` when artisan was started with `--cdp-port` (CLI-only, not this MCP tool).

*Gestures (pointer actions)*
- `dusk_tap` (`ext.dusk.tap`): Tap a widget (Down + 50ms hold + Up at the LIVE ref center, re-resolved post-gate). Opt-in `verify:true` adds a `changed` boolean reporting whether the tap had an observable effect. Opt-in `until:"<text>"` polls (up to `untilTimeoutMs`, default 3000) for that text post-tap and adds an `untilMatched` boolean.
- `dusk_hover` (`ext.dusk.hover`): Hover mouse cursor over a widget (PointerHoverEvent).
- `dusk_drag` (`ext.dusk.drag`): Drag from one widget to another (Down + 5x Move + Up).
- `dusk_dblclick` (`ext.dusk.dblclick`): Double-click a widget (two tap sequences ~100ms apart).
- `dusk_right_click` (`ext.dusk.right_click`): Fire secondary (right) mouse button click.
- `dusk_triple_click` (`ext.dusk.triple_click`): Fire three primary clicks (~100ms apart each).

*Text Input*
- `dusk_type` (`ext.dusk.type`): Type text into a TextField (calls userUpdateTextEditingValue).
- `dusk_clear` (`ext.dusk.clear`): Clear TextEditingController backing a text field.
- `dusk_fill` (`ext.dusk.fill`): Focus + clear + type + settle a text field in one call; composes the gated focus/clear/type handlers and retries once on a stale handle. Replaces the manual fill dance.

*Scrolling*
- `dusk_scroll` (`ext.dusk.scroll`): Scroll a Scrollable widget by direction + pixels.

*Waits / Polling*
- `dusk_wait_for` (`ext.dusk.wait_for`): Wait until text/textGone/expression condition met or timeout.
- `dusk_wait_for_network_idle` (`ext.dusk.wait_for_network_idle`): Wait until in-flight HTTP count reaches 0 + idle window.

*Navigation*
- `dusk_navigate` (`ext.dusk.navigate`): Push a named route onto the active router.
- `dusk_navigate_back` (`ext.dusk.navigate_back`): Pop the top route off navigator stack.
- `dusk_get_routes` (`ext.dusk.get_routes`): Enumerate declared routes in the active router.
- `dusk_dismiss_modals` (`ext.dusk.dismiss_modals`): Pop every PopupRoute (dialog, bottom sheet, popup menu) across all NavigatorState instances; returns additive `popped` count.
- `dusk_reset_overlays` (`ext.dusk.reset_overlays`): Return to a clean screen via 3 idempotent layers: dismiss all PopupRoutes, press Escape, then tap a Cancel/Dismiss/Close/OK/Done affordance. Returns `{popped, escaped, dismissTapped}`.

*Find (Query Handles)*
- `dusk_find` (`ext.dusk.find`): Find widget by text/semanticsLabel/key, return re-resolvable `q<N>` handle.

*Diagnostics*
- `dusk_console` (`ext.dusk.console`): Read recent log entries. Returns merged union of in-package `debugPrint` capture buffer (captures `debugPrint()`, `print()`, and any Flutter framework path through the `debugPrint` callback; does NOT capture direct `developer.log()` calls) and telescope reader when wired; deduped, newest-first. Works without telescope.
- `dusk_exceptions` (`ext.dusk.exceptions`): Read recent exception entries. Returns merged union of in-package non-fatal `FlutterError` capture buffer (incl. overflow tagged `type: "overflow"`) and telescope reader; deduped, newest-first. Optional `since` ISO8601 param filters to entries strictly after that timestamp (for before/after action deltas).
- `dusk_observe` (`ext.dusk.observe`): Return structured candidate list of every interactive widget on screen.

*Keyboard / Input*
- `dusk_press_key` (`ext.dusk.press_key`): Press hardware key with optional modifiers (control, shift, alt, meta).
- `dusk_focus` (`ext.dusk.focus`): Request keyboard focus on widget identified by ref.
- `dusk_blur` (`ext.dusk.blur`): Clear keyboard focus from current focus holder.

*Form Controls*
- `dusk_select_option` (`ext.dusk.select_option`): Select option in DropdownButton by ref + value.
- `dusk_set_checkbox` (`ext.dusk.set_checkbox`): Set checked state of Checkbox or Switch widget.

*Evaluate (MCP-only; no CLI mirror)*
- `dusk_evaluate` (`ext.dusk.evaluate`): Evaluate Dart expression in running app isolate via Tinker bridge.

*App Control*
- `dusk_close_app` (`ext.dusk.close_app`): Request graceful shutdown via `SystemNavigator.pop()`.

*Hot Reload (substrate-routed)*
- `dusk_hot_reload_and_snap` (`artisan:dusk:hot_reload_and_snap`): Hot reload app, capture snapshot + screenshot + recent exceptions in one round-trip.

*Viewport / Device Emulation (substrate-routed, CDP)*
- `dusk_resize_viewport` (`artisan:dusk:resize`): Resize Flutter web viewport via Chrome DevTools Protocol.
- `dusk_device_profile` (`artisan:dusk:device`): Emulate named device preset via CDP.

## Docs
- [Installation](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/getting-started/installation.md)
- [Quickstart](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/getting-started/quickstart.md)
- [Commands index](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/index.md)
- [Driving real apps: gotchas for agents](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/getting-started/driving-real-apps-gotchas.md)
- [dusk:snap](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-snap.md)
- [dusk:tap](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-tap.md)
- [dusk:fill](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-fill.md)
- [dusk:reset_overlays](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-reset-overlays.md)
- [dusk:find](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-find.md)
- [dusk:observe](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-observe.md)
- [dusk:screenshot](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-screenshot.md)
- [dusk:doctor](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-doctor.md)
- [dusk:install](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/commands/dusk-install.md)
- [Actionability gate](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/reference/actionability-gate.md)

## MCP
- [MCP overview](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/mcp/overview.md)
- [MCP setup (.mcp.json wiring)](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/mcp/setup.md): when fastcli is absent, `mcp:install` auto-selects `dart run fluttersdk_dusk mcp:serve` as the plugin-aware fallback (no manual edit needed).
- [MCP tool reference (33 tools)](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/mcp/tool-reference.md)

## Plugins
- [Magic integration](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/plugins/magic-integration.md)
- [Wind integration](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/plugins/wind-integration.md)
- [Authoring a snapshot enricher](https://fluttersdk.com/dusk/plugins/enricher-authoring.md)

## AI & Tooling
- [fluttersdk/ai](https://github.com/fluttersdk/ai): canonical distribution channel. One-command install via `npx skills add fluttersdk/ai --skill fluttersdk-dusk` covers Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, VS Code Copilot, Codex CLI, Cline, Roo Code.
- [Dusk skill source](https://github.com/fluttersdk/dusk/tree/master/skills/fluttersdk-dusk): canonical `SKILL.md` + references (`mcp-tools.md`, `cli-commands.md`, `actionability-and-refs.md`, `workflows.md`, `community.md`); `fluttersdk/ai` mirrors this directory.
- [Hosted docs MCP server](https://mcp.fluttersdk.com): Streamable HTTP, single `search-docs` tool over the dusk docs corpus, no auth required.
- [Stdio MCP bridge](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@fluttersdk/mcp): `npx @fluttersdk/mcp` for clients without HTTP MCP transport.
